channel.title: Security on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Security on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: github: Upcoming GHES change impacting uploading support bundles
item.description:
GitHub will begin rejecting some GHES support bundle uploads starting August 18, 2026 because of an upcoming security change. Administrators should review the new upload requirements before that date to avoid failed submissions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Least privilege for AI agents: Identity, access, and tool binding
item.description:
Microsoft Security published a post on applying least-privilege principles to AI agents, focusing on identity, access control, tool binding, and auditing as agents become more autonomous.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Unpacking the AsyncAPI npm supply chain compromise and import-time payload delivery
item.description:
Microsoft reports that attackers compromised AsyncAPI npm packages and used trusted CI/CD workflows to distribute malware via npm. The writeup details the attack chain, import-time payload delivery, and suggested defenses.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Improvements to secret scanning and public monitoring
item.description:
GitHub rolled out updates to secret scanning and public monitoring. Resend is now a secret scanning partner, and secret scanning can detect new secret types from APIclub.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Code scanning shows AI security detections on pull requests
item.description:
GitHub code scanning now shows AI-powered security detections on pull requests. The feature expands coverage to languages and frameworks not currently supported by CodeQL.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Manage secret scanning custom patterns via REST API
item.description:
GitHub now supports managing secret scanning custom patterns through the REST API. The new generally available endpoints let security teams list, create, and edit custom patterns programmatically.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Securing our future: July 2026 progress report on Microsoft’s Secure Future Initiative
item.description:
Microsoft published its July 2026 Secure Future Initiative progress report, summarizing work on secure foundations, AI-assisted defense, and broader cybersecurity readiness. The provided text is only a brief overview and does not include specific implementation details or metrics.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Protecting Microsoft at AI speed: How SFI proactively hardens our cloud
item.description:
Microsoft says its Secure Future Initiative (SFI) uses security requirements, threat intelligence, and operational frameworks to define what a well-defended cloud service should look like. It emphasizes continuously evaluating live services against those requirements at AI speed.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: npm install-time security and GAT bypass2fa deprecation
item.description:
npm v12 is now generally available and tagged latest. It enables the install-time security defaults announced in June and starts deprecating GAT bypass2fa.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Exceptions in WAF for Azure Application Gateway and Azure Front Door
item.description:
Azure Web Application Firewall now has an exceptions feature in public preview for Azure Application Gateway and Azure Front Door. It lets you allow specific requests that would otherwise be blocked as false positives, complementing existing exclusions.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Secret scanning extended metadata and multipart validation
item.description:
GitHub secret scanning now surfaces extended metadata for supported secret types to help identify ownership and impact of leaked secrets. The update also includes general availability of extended metadata checks and multipart validation support.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Improving security posture across the Microsoft partner ecosystem
item.description:
Microsoft says it is improving security across its partner ecosystem by tightening CSP vetting, applying least-privilege access, increasing monitoring, and promoting risk-management best practices. The post is framed as guidance for partners rather than a product release.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Secret scanning public monitoring for enterprises
item.description:
GitHub announced secret scanning public monitoring for enterprises, aimed at helping organizations detect and respond to secret leaks as soon as they appear publicly. The post is a brief announcement and does not include implementation details in the provided text.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning adds validators for Asana, IBM, and MessageBird
item.description:
GitHub secret scanning now validates Asana, IBM, and MessageBird secrets to help determine whether detected credentials are still active. The update adds validity checks for these secret types.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Accelerating the quantum-safe timeline
item.description:
Microsoft says it is accelerating quantum-safe readiness and urging organizations to begin transitioning earlier as the perceived risk timeline shifts. The post is a brief announcement pointing readers to guidance on what can be done now.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Read-only Actions cache for untrusted triggers
item.description:
GitHub Actions now uses read-only cache tokens on the default branch for workflow events that can be triggered without repository write permissions. This narrows cache access for untrusted triggers by applying least privilege.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Photo ZIP campaign targeting hospitality industry delivers Node.js implant for persistent access
item.description:
Microsoft Threat Intelligence reported an active multi-stage intrusion campaign targeting hospitality organizations in Europe and Asia. The attackers use photo-themed ZIP archives and fake image shortcut files to deploy a persistent Node.js implant and avoid detection.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: npm adds preventive account protection for high-impact accounts
item.description:
npm now applies a temporary preventive safeguard to high-impact accounts when it detects sensitive account changes. The measure is intended to reduce account-takeover risk for maintainers of widely used packages.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Microsoft a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Endpoint Management Platforms
item.description:
Microsoft says it was named a Leader in Forrester Wave: Endpoint Management Platforms, Q2 2026, and reports the highest scores in the current offering and strategy categories.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Self-service credential revocation for incident response
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise owners can now use a new break-glass capability to immediately revoke all credentials for a compromised account or stolen credentials during incident response. The feature is self-service and intended for urgent security handling.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning adds extended metadata for Replicate secrets
item.description:
GitHub Secret Scanning now adds extended metadata when it detects Replicate secrets, giving more context about leaked credentials. The change applies to the Replicate secret pattern and is meant to improve incident response and triage.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: AutoJack: How a single page can RCE the host running your AI agent
item.description:
Microsoft describes AutoJack, an exploit chain where a single malicious webpage can cause remote code execution on the host running an AI browsing agent. The attack abuses localhost trust, missing authentication, and unsafe parameter handling in AutoGen Studio’s MCP WebSocket.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Safer pull_request_target defaults for GitHub Actions checkout
item.description:
GitHub is changing the default behavior of the Actions checkout action for workflows triggered by pull_request_target to reduce common security mistakes. The goal is to avoid exposing base-branch credentials or secrets to untrusted pull request code.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning updates – June 2026
item.description:
GitHub added secret scanning coverage in June 2026 with new partner integrations, more default push-protection blocks, additional validity checks, and richer metadata for leaked secrets.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: AI is accelerating cyberattacks—here’s how to stay ahead
item.description:
Microsoft describes how its identity and security products use unified signals to help teams prevent, detect, and respond to AI-accelerated attacks faster. The post is a security-focused overview rather than a feature release.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: commandline: Information-flow control: Moving toward secure, autonomous agents
item.description:
The article says information-flow control (IFC) is a deterministic security approach that could help make autonomous agents more secure. It presents IFC as a path toward controlling how information moves through agent systems.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: security: Microsoft Defender email security benchmarking: Key insights from one year of data
item.description:
Microsoft published a blog post summarizing one year of real-world email security benchmarking for Microsoft Defender, comparing its performance against SEG and ICES vendors. The provided excerpt does not include the specific results or metrics.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Bot-created pull requests can run workflows if approved
item.description:
Pull requests created by github-actions[bot] can now run CI/CD workflows after a user approves them. The approval step is required as a security check before generated code is allowed to trigger workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming breaking changes for npm v12
item.description:
npm v12 will change the default behavior of npm install with security-related defaults. The post says these changes are already available as warnings in npm 11.16.0 and newer.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Periodic code scanning of inactive repositories
item.description:
GitHub code scanning now supports scheduled security scans for repositories with no pushes or pull requests for six months or longer. This helps organizations keep inactive repositories under periodic review.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Microsoft Foundry agent security capabilities in Microsoft Defender for Cloud are transitioning to a Microsoft Agent 365 license
item.description:
Starting July 1, 2026, Microsoft Foundry agent security capabilities in Microsoft Defender for Cloud will move to a Microsoft Agent 365 license. Agent protection in Defender will use Agent 365 observability logs.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Securing CI/CD in an agentic world: Claude Code Github action case
item.description:
Microsoft Threat Intelligence reported a prompt injection path in Claude Code GitHub Action that could expose workflow secrets under specific conditions. The post describes the attack chain, disclosure, Anthropic’s mitigation, and recommendations for securing AI-assisted CI/CD workflows.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Safe key rotation for Azure Cosmos DB
item.description:
Azure Cosmos DB now has a public preview feature for safe key rotation. It shows when each account key was last used so you can avoid rotating a key that is still in use by applications.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Confidential Compute support on Azure Container Apps
item.description:
Azure Container Apps now generally supports Confidential Compute for regulated or sensitive containerized workloads. The feature adds stronger isolation and protection for data while it is being processed.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Cloud and local sandboxes for GitHub Copilot now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot now supports secure, isolated sandboxes for tool execution in both local and cloud environments, available in public preview. The update is intended to isolate Copilot actions from the rest of the system.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Microsoft Build 2026: Securing code, agents, and models across the development lifecycle
item.description:
Microsoft says Build 2026 introduced MDASH and additional security capabilities aimed at securing code, agents, and models across the development lifecycle. The post frames these updates as support for fast AI development with security controls built in.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: ai: Build agents you can trust across any framework with open evals and a control standard
item.description:
Microsoft describes a set of tools for building AI agents with open evaluations, portable runtime controls, observability, and security workflows that are intended to work across frameworks. The post focuses on trust and control for agent development rather than a single framework-specific feature set.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: security: Malicious npm packages abuse dependency confusion to profile developer environments
item.description:
Microsoft reports a dependency confusion campaign using 33 malicious npm packages to collect reconnaissance data from developer and build environments. The post outlines the attack chain, attacker tradecraft, and detection opportunities for identifying related activity.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Microsoft is named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Endpoint Protection
item.description:
Microsoft says it was named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Endpoint Protection. The post provides no product changes, feature updates, or technical release details.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: CodeQL 2.25.5 improves query accuracy for GitHub Actions
item.description:
CodeQL 2.25.5 was released with query accuracy improvements for GitHub Actions, along with additional accuracy improvements across C/C++.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: k8s: Reconciling the Past: Correcting Records for Unfixed Kubernetes CVEs
item.description:
Kubernetes will correct several older CVE records on June 1, 2026 so they no longer imply fixed versions for issues that remain unfixed by design. The update affects CVE-2020-8561, CVE-2020-8562, CVE-2021-25740, and a formatting update for CVE-2020-8554; admins may see new scanner findings after the records are corrected.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Virtual network flow logs connector with Microsoft Sentinel
item.description:
Azure virtual network flow logs connector with Microsoft Sentinel is now generally available. It lets you export and analyze network traffic data in Microsoft Sentinel security workflows.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Microsoft recognized as a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Workforce Identity Security Platforms
item.description:
Microsoft says it was named a Leader in The Forrester Wave: Workforce Identity Security Platforms, Q2 2026, with the highest scores in both current offering and strategy. The post does not include product changes or technical release details.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Microsoft Security success stories: How St. Luke’s and ManpowerGroup are securing AI foundations
item.description:
Microsoft highlights how St. Luke’s and ManpowerGroup are using governance, identity, and cloud security controls to support AI adoption at scale. The post frames security as part of the foundation for AI deployment rather than a separate concern.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: Retirement: TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 in Azure App Service, Azure Functions, and Azure Logic Apps
item.description:
Azure App Service, Azure Functions, and Azure Logic Apps will stop accepting TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 connections after May 31, 2027. Clients and services must use TLS 1.2 or later to continue connecting.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Mini Shai Hulud: Compromised @antv npm packages enable CI/CD credential theft
item.description:
Microsoft reports that compromised @antv npm packages were used to deploy the Mini Shai-Hulud payload, which runs during npm install and steals CI/CD secrets from Linux-based automation environments. The malware targets credentials from GitHub, AWS, Kubernetes, Vault, npm, and 1Password.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Securing the gaming culture of cultures
item.description:
The post says gaming platforms and communities have unique security challenges and discusses ways to better protect them. It is a Microsoft Security Blog article focused on securing gaming culture and platforms.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Introducing RAMPART and Clarity: Open source tools to bring safety into Agent development workflow
item.description:
Microsoft announced two open-source tools, RAMPART and Clarity, aimed at adding safety checks to agent development workflows. The post frames the need around enterprise AI agents that can access data, execute code, and take actions across connected systems.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: How to better protect your growing business in an AI-powered world
item.description:
Microsoft Security Blog says growing businesses should use built-in security to help keep operations running, protect customer trust, and support growth in an AI-driven environment.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: Deprecation and removal of Service ExternalIPs
item.description:
Kubernetes 1.36 formally deprecates Service.spec.externalIPs because the feature is insecure by default and can enable CVE-2020-8554-style abuse. Kubernetes will warn on use now, disable it in kube-proxy no earlier than v1.40 with an opt-in path, and remove opt-back-in support no earlier than v1.43.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: security: Defense in depth for autonomous AI agents
item.description:
Microsoft says autonomous AI agents require updated defense-in-depth controls centered on application-layer design, identity, and human oversight. The post frames agent security as an evolution of existing defense-in-depth practices rather than a new model.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Defense at AI speed: Microsoft’s new multi-model agentic security system tops leading industry benchmark
item.description:
Microsoft announced MDASH, a new multi-model agentic scanning harness for AI-powered cyber defense. The post says it topped a leading industry benchmark, but provides no technical details in the excerpt.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Defending consumer web properties against modern DDoS attacks
item.description:
Microsoft published guidance on defending consumer web properties against modern DDoS attacks, emphasizing layered security, resilient architecture, and graceful service degradation.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: When prompts become shells: RCE vulnerabilities in AI agent frameworks
item.description:
Microsoft says new research found prompt injection issues in AI agent frameworks can lead to remote code execution. The post outlines affected areas and recommends securing agents against these flaws.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: World Passkey Day: Advancing passwordless authentication
item.description:
Microsoft says it is advancing passkey adoption as a replacement for passwords, with the goals of reducing phishing risk and simplifying sign-in. The post is framed around World Passkey Day and passwordless authentication.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Dependency scanning with GitHub MCP Server is in public preview
item.description:
GitHub MCP Server now supports dependency scanning in public preview. It can scan code changes for vulnerable dependencies before you commit or open a pull request.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Code-to-cloud risk visibility with Microsoft Defender for Cloud is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub says the code-to-cloud risk visibility integration with Microsoft Defender for Cloud is now generally available. The release includes follow-up improvements since public preview, bringing artifact and runtime context closer together.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: aks: Release 2026-04-28
item.description:
AKS 2026-04-28 adds new patch releases for Kubernetes 1.33–1.35, introduces several preview features, and updates multiple platform components and node images. It also highlights a security mitigation for CVE-2026-31431, deprecates Kubernetes 1.29 LTS, and starts moving Automatic clusters toward Gateway API-based ingress.

  • Source: Release notes from AKS

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: Admission Policies That Can't Be Deleted
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 adds an alpha manifest-based admission control feature that loads admission webhooks and CEL policies from files on disk at API server startup. It closes the bootstrap gap and allows on-disk policies to protect API-based admission resources from deletion or modification.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Prefix-scoped access for User Delegation SAS
item.description:
Prefix-scoped access for User Delegation SAS in Azure Blob Storage is now generally available in all Azure regions. It adds a scope between container-level and single-blob access for SAS tokens.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: Governing MCP tool calls in .NET with the Agent Governance Toolkit
item.description:
The post describes how to govern MCP tool calls in .NET using the Agent Governance Toolkit. It focuses on policy-based access control, security scanning, and response sanitization to reduce risk in AI agent systems.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: Fine-Grained Kubelet API Authorization Graduates to GA
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 makes fine-grained kubelet API authorization GA. KubeletFineGrainedAuthz is now always enabled, allowing RBAC to target specific kubelet subresources like nodes/metrics and nodes/stats instead of the broad nodes/proxy permission.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Hosted Agents in Foundry Agent Service
item.description:
Azure Foundry Agent Service now offers hosted agents in public preview. Each agent session runs in an isolated sandbox with a clean environment, no shared state, and strong compute boundaries.

  • Source: Azure Updates
